metadata.title: Awards, Honours & Persons in News Current Affairs MCQs

questions.question: Who won the 2025 PEN Pinter Prize?
questions.explanation: Recently, Leila Aboulela, a Sudanese-Scottish author, won the 2025 PEN Pinter Prize for her bold and truthful literary voice. The award was announced at English PEN’s summer party in London, with live readings of her work by actors Khalid Abdalla and Amira Ghazalla. She writes from a Muslim and immigrant perspective, exploring faith, migration, and cultural identity. Aboulela will receive the prize on October 10 at the British Library, where she will also announce the Writer of Courage for 2025. Past Writers of Courage include Alaa Abd el-Fattah (2024) and Rahile Dawut (2023), both persecuted for defending free speech.

questions.question: Which Indian won the 2025 United Nations Population Award for promoting gender equality?
questions.explanation: Recently, Indian women’s rights activist Varsha Deshpande received the 2025 United Nations Population Award in the individual category for promoting gender equality and reproductive rights. She is the secretary of Dalit Mahila Vikas Mandal, a Maharashtra-based organisation she founded in 1990 to empower marginalised women. The award includes a gold medal, diploma, and monetary prize.

questions.question: Who has been appointed as the Chairperson of the National Financial Reporting Authority (NFRA) in July 2025?
questions.explanation: The government has recently appointed Nitin Gupta, former Chairman of the Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT), as the new Chairperson of the National Financial Reporting Authority (NFRA). NFRA was established in 2018 by the Government of India under the Companies Act, 2013. It is headquartered in New Delhi. NFRA recommends accounting and auditing policies and standards for companies. It monitors and ensures compliance with these standards across companies. It oversees the quality of services provided by professionals in the accounting and auditing field.

questions.question: Which mobile application won the World Summit on the Information Society (WSIS) Prizes 2025 Champion Award for rural digital governance??
questions.explanation: The Meri Panchayat mobile app recently won the prestigious World Summit on the Information Society (WSIS) Prizes 2025 Champion Award. The award was given under the category “Cultural Diversity and Identity, Linguistic Diversity and Local Content” at the WSIS+20 High-Level Event held in Geneva, Switzerland. Meri Panchayat App is developed by the Ministry of Panchayati Raj and National Informatics Centre (NIC) under the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology. The app provides real-time access to Panchayat budgets, payments, development plans, and details of elected representatives.

questions.question: Who has become the first Indian to win the title of ‘Best Magic Creator 2025’ at the FISM World Championship of Magic?
questions.explanation: Suhani Shah from Rajasthan recently won the title of ‘Best Magic Creator 2025’ at the Federation Internationale des Societes Magiques (FISM) World Championship of Magic. She became the first Indian ever to win this prestigious global award in the online magic category. The FISM World Championship of Magic, known as the Olympics of Magic, was held in Torino, Italy from July 14 to 19, 2025. FISM, founded in 1948, is held every three years and features stage magic, close-up magic, and online magic.

questions.question: Who has been appointed as the new Chairman of the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI) in July 2025?
questions.explanation: Recently, on July 24, 2025, the government appointed Ajay Seth as the new Chairman of the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI). The appointment was approved by the Appointments Committee of the Cabinet. Ajay Seth is a 1987-batch Indian Administrative Service (IAS) officer from the Karnataka cadre. He retired as Union Finance and Economic Affairs Secretary in June 2025. He will serve as IRDAI Chairman for three years or till the age of 65 or until further orders, whichever comes earlier. He succeeds Debasish Panda, also a former IAS officer, whose term ended in March 2025.

questions.question: Who was honoured with the G.P. Birla Memorial Award 2025?
questions.explanation: Recently, G P Birla Memorial Award was conferred on V. Narayanan, Chairman of Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) and Secretary of the Department of Space. The award was presented by Nirmala Birla, Chairperson of G P Birla Archaeological Astronomical and Scientific Institute. The award has a prestigious legacy and has earlier been given to 32 Nobel laureates. Past recipients include Nobel Prize winner Venkataraman Ramakrishnan, physicist Jogesh Pati, former President A. P. J. Abdul Kalam, and former ISRO chief Kasturirangan. The award recognises global leaders for their contributions to scientific progress and innovation.

questions.question: Who was honoured with the 43rd Lokmanya Tilak National Award 2025?
questions.explanation: Recently, Union Minister Nitin Gadkari received the 43rd Lokmanya Tilak National Award in Pune. The award was instituted in 1983 by the Tilak Smarak Mandir Trust. It is given annually on 1st August, the death anniversary of Lokmanya Tilak. The award honours individuals who have made extraordinary contributions to the progress and development of the nation. Lokmanya Tilak was born as Keshav Gangadhar Tilak and was a key figure in India’s freedom struggle. He was a nationalist, social reformer, and strong advocate of Swaraj or Self-Rule.

questions.question: Who has been appointed as new chief secretary of Bihar in August 2025?
questions.explanation: Pratyaya Amrit, a 1991-batch Indian Administrative Service (IAS) officer, has been appointed as the new Chief Secretary of Bihar. He is currently serving as Development Commissioner and also holds additional charge of Health and Disaster Management Department. He will replace Amrit Lal Meena, a 1989-batch IAS officer, who is set to retire on August 31, 2025. Pratyaya will assume charge as Chief Secretary on September 1, 2025. Till then, he will serve as Officer on Special Duty (OSD) in the Chief Secretary’s office.

questions.question: Which Indian group won the UNDP Equator Initiative Award 2025?
questions.explanation: The Bibi Fatima Women’s Self-Help Group (SHG) from Dharwad district won the Equator Initiative Award 2025. The award is presented every two years by the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) under the Equator Initiative. It supports Indigenous Peoples and local communities in tackling land degradation, conserving biodiversity, and improving livelihoods. The award is often called the Nobel Prize for Biodiversity Conservation. Bibi Fatima SHG introduced millet-based mixed cropping on rainfed farms. They promoted climate-resilient farming, livestock rearing, horticulture, and popularised millets.
